steps.title: Identify the study limitation
steps.content: The study surveyed respondents from Serbia, which the passage identifies as a country with a developing economy. Because the participants all came from one national and economic context, the researchers warned against applying the results to customers generally.

steps.title: Complete the caution
steps.content:
• Choice A: This applies the Serbian survey results broadly to online retailers in many countries, which is exactly the kind of generalization the researchers cautioned against.
  • Choice B: This fits the caution because it calls for additional research with participants from countries at different levels of economic development before drawing a broad conclusion about delivery time, tracking, and package design.
  • Choice C: This focuses only on whether the same result would appear in a larger Serbian sample, but the stated limitation concerns applying the findings beyond Serbia.
  • Choice D: This makes a specific prediction about customers in countries with higher economic development, but the passage gives no evidence for that prediction.
The answer is B.
